findingVerification.notes: The benchmark warden.toml explicitly sets defaults.verification.enabled = true.

traceCapture.notes: Run used --traces on all six clean shards. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.

timing.notes: Six shards completed sequentially with standard parallelism 4. No provider errors, retries, repairs, host sleep, or manual interruption occurred.

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count; merged findings can map to multiple corpus IDs.

scores.notes: Internal EA features endpoint is reachable by any authenticated user.
Cleartext option logging is not the allowlist tuple corpus bug at this commit.
Empty project scope exposes release thresholds across organizations.
UserInfo accepts tokens for inactive users and OAuth applications.
Signed-cookie password persistence is not the SUPERUSER_ORG_ID fail-open corpus bug.
Invite-token logging is not the arbitrary-member-deletion corpus bug.
Accept-invite deletes an arbitrary organization member without validating the token.
Merge-SHA label approval TOCTOU is not the newline-based GITHUB_OUTPUT injection corpus bug.
Deterministic OAuth state is not the unknown GitHub installation acceptance corpus bug.
client_secret_jwt does not require exp and can be replayed after the short jti cache expires.
Fresh OAuth client secrets are exposed to non-owner grantees.
Bitbucket Server accepts unsigned public webhooks.
Timing-unsafe webhook comparisons are not the Statsig timestamp-freshness corpus bug.
Statsig's signed timestamp is never checked for freshness.
A live Jira Server webhook JWT is written to logs.
Legacy GitHub Enterprise signature bypass trusts a caller-controlled host header.
Release webhook token uses predictable uuid1().hex material.
Size-analysis comparison download is scoped by organization rather than project.
Missing Seer RPC rate limiting is not the project-access corpus bug.
Mailgun's signed timestamp is accepted without freshness or replay protection.
Jira autocomplete escapes quotes but not backslashes, enabling JQL injection.
Legacy Jira requests disable TLS verification while sending Basic Auth.
Organization-level Seer RPC methods expose project-backed data without project access checks.
Copy-as-curl leaves HTTP methods and header names unescaped.
Same ECharts tooltip HTML injection pattern across feature flags, mobile builds, and the app-size treemap.
CSV macro sanitization misses tab and carriage-return prefixes.
Legacy OAuth prefix matching accepts an attacker-controlled redirect URI.
Relocation export secret retention is not the legacy OAuth redirect-prefix corpus bug.
SSO override excludes an OrganizationMemberMapping by the wrong primary key.
Discover and Explore visit endpoints both skip object-level saved-query permission checks.
Arbitrary member deletion is not the shared-issue slug-confusion corpus bug at this commit.
Read-only members can plant the first flag-webhook signing secret.
Partial webhook-secret disclosure is distinct from first-secret creation authorization.
Perforce install-probe SSRF is not the configData password-exposure corpus bug.
Perforce configData exposes the stored password or ticket to org:read members.
Slack Seer autofix membership bypass is not either same-file authorization corpus bug.
Raw preprod error disclosure is not the cross-project artifact-access corpus bug.
Any authenticated user can trigger attacker-selected GCS transfer jobs.
Seer coding-agent handoff does not bind run_id to the requested group.
Account-merge verification-code expiry is never enforced.
Workflow updates remove detector connections without checking project permissions.
Unbounded root-cause-analysis pagination is not the transaction filter-injection corpus bug.
System-options GET password disclosure is not the cleartext option logging corpus bug.
System-options PUT logs credential values in cleartext.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one clean Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime pi --model openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k3 --effort max --traces. Each shard analyzes every unique corpus path for that SHA, not the whole Sentry repository. All 156 chunks completed successfully with complete traces and no repair artifacts. The run emitted 44 verified findings. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.
